buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller cut Amazon 94%, rotating into semis, copper, Argentina — AI infra over mega-cap software.
Dalio: US equities near 1929/2000 bubble levels; unsustainable debt; shift to boring fundamental trades.
Gundlach: Warsh no easy-money Fed; 1970s inflation challenge; higher yields persist through 2026.
Tudor Jones: S&P buyers risk decade of losses; long Bitcoin and AI infra as inflation hedges.
Burry: shorting Nvidia, rotating into undervalued healthcare; SpaceX at $3T grossly overvalued.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): biggest bubble since railroads; only 4% of S&P at new highs; imminent rally threat.
Rosenberg: stagflation incoming — inflation higher, growth lower, large parts of economy in deep pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — tech exuberance colliding with recession risk; crash parallels flashing.
Yardeni: bull intact through 2028; FEMO driving gains, not speculation; corrections are healthy.
Hatzius (GS): recession risk only 15%; rate hikes off table; US outgrows Europe materially.
whats_new: Druckenmiller executed 94% Amazon exit this period — sharpest mega-cap tech rotation signal in the dataset.
Tepper cut Microsoft 82% simultaneously; two elite buy-side managers dumping large-cap tech in same period.
Ackman flipped: buying Microsoft aggressively while Tepper exits — direct intra-buy-side contradiction emerging.
Hatzius formally cut US recession probability to 15%, most explicit probability anchor in current period.
Warsh Fed narrative crystallised: Gundlach and Druckenmiller both flagging hawkish policy surprise risk.
vs_market_buy: Dalio/Tudor Jones call 1929-2000 bubble; market still pricing ~21x forward PE with no valuation compression.
Gundlach expects yields higher all year; market rate-cut expectations price 1-2 cuts by year-end.
Burry short Nvidia while consensus treats it as essential infrastructure; stock near all-time highs.
Druckenmiller exits Amazon as market continues pricing hyperscaler FCF ramp; Berezin calls those projections unrealistic.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees stagflation; credit spreads near 2-year tights imply no stress, contradicting his thesis directly.
Hartnett flags only 4% of S&P at new highs yet index near record — breadth divergence market ignores.
Reid warns of crash parallels; VIX and risk premiums remain compressed, implying market dismisses tail risk.
Berezin fades hyperscaler FCF projections; market capitalising $700B 2030 FCF as if already deliverable.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Slok (sell): economy hot, no cuts needed from strength. Gundlach (buy): no cuts because inflation won't allow it — same outcome, opposite macro diagnosis.
Yardeni (sell): rally is earnings-driven, no bubble. Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy): explicit 1929/2000 bubble call — sharpest valuation disagreement in the set.
Visser (sell): buy AI mega-cap infra, NVIDIA/Oracle/Amazon. Burry (buy): short Nvidia, fade growth — direct sector opposition.
Hatzius (sell): 15% recession risk, US keeps growing. Rosenberg (sell): stagflation, growth decelerating — rare sell-side vs sell-side macro split.
aligned: Dalio + Hartnett + Tudor Jones + Reid all independently invoke 1999-2000 dot-com parallel; market continues rallying regardless.
Gundlach + Druckenmiller + Wilson all flag sticky inflation and Fed hawkishness as the critical near-term market hurdle.
Druckenmiller + Burry + Ackman + Berezin all rotating away from obvious mega-cap AI/tech — consensus trim despite different targets.
Marks + Sonders + Reid all warn investor complacency is dangerously elevated relative to underlying macro fragility.
buy_side_trades: Reduce/short mega-cap tech (Nvidia, Amazon, Microsoft). Source: Druckenmiller, Burry, Tepper, Ackman (Alphabet).
Long Bitcoin and gold as inflation hedges against persistent rates and dollar risk. Source: Tudor Jones, Gundlach.
Long semiconductors, copper, Argentine equities as AI infra and EM reform plays. Source: Druckenmiller.
Long AI memory and infrastructure over software mega-caps for better risk-reward. Source: Tepper, Tudor Jones (IREN).
Long undervalued healthcare; shift to quality fundamentals over speculative growth. Source: Burry, Ackman.
sell_side_trades: Stronger US dollar positioning as most unexpected macro bet of H2 2026. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Long US equities over European equities on divergent growth trajectories. Source: Hatzius (GS).
Long AI chip infrastructure (NVIDIA, Oracle, Amazon) and high-growth biotech. Source: Visser (22V).
Long duration bonds given rate hikes off the table and recession risk contained. Source: Hatzius (GS).
Long safe dividend-paying stocks; long Iran peace deal beneficiaries (oil, defense). Source: Hartnett (BofA).
dominant_themes: AI bubble valuation vs earnings-driven legitimacy
Fed path under Warsh — hawkish surprise risk
Mega-cap tech rotation into semis, infra, EM
Stagflation vs hot economy macro split
Geopolitical risk premium (Iran) as fragile relief trade

buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller dumped 94% of Amazon, rotating into semis, copper, and Argentine equities — away from AI mega-cap.
Dalio: US equities approaching 1929/2000 bubble levels; national debt unsustainable; cut risk, go boring.
Gundlach: Warsh is no dove — Fed faces 1970s inflation, higher yields persist through 2026.
Tudor Jones: S&P 500 buyers risk a lost decade; long Bitcoin and AI infra instead of broad equities.
Burry short Nvidia, long undervalued healthcare — sees fundamental disconnect in growth/tech valuations.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): AI rally is the biggest bubble since railroads; only 4% of S&P 500 at new highs — breadth screaming March 2000.
Rosenberg: stagflation confirmed — inflation rising, growth decelerating, large parts of economy in deep pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — S&P rally mirrors pre-crash setups; geopolitical ceasefire relief is fragile.
Yardeni: bull case intact through 2028; FEMO (earnings momentum) not a bubble, corrections are healthy.
Slok (Apollo): economy running hot, zero evidence AI is killing jobs — strength argues against cuts.
whats_new: Druckenmiller made a decisive mega-cap exit this week — 94% Amazon cut signals a regime-level portfolio shift, not tactical trim.
Gundlach explicitly reframed Warsh as a 1970s-style hawk, upgrading Fed hawkishness from risk to base case.
Ackman pivoted all-in on Microsoft while dumping Alphabet — quality-over-speculation rotation crystallised into a trade.
Tepper slashed Microsoft 82% and rotated into AI memory stocks — contradicts Ackman's quality thesis on the same name.
vs_market_buy: Dalio/Tudor Jones call bubble near 1929/2000 extremes — market is pricing 21-22x forward PE with no valuation concession.
Gundlach sees persistent higher rates; market is still pricing 1-2 cuts by year-end, a significant gap.
Burry short Nvidia while market bids AI semis to peak multiples — direct clash on AI monetisation timeline.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees stagflation; investment-grade credit spreads near 2-year tights imply no growth stress whatsoever.
Hartnett warns of imminent rally-ending catalyst; fund flows and momentum indicators still support risk-on positioning.
Reid flags crash-analogue setups; options market implied vol remains subdued, pricing complacency not tail risk.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Gundlach (buy-side) says Fed hawkishness is the dominant macro story; Slok (sell-side) says strong economy justifies no cuts — same rate outcome, polar opposite causal logic.
Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy-side) call 2000-style bubble demanding de-risking; Yardeni/Visser (sell-side) say earnings momentum makes valuations rational — irreconcilable on the same market level.
Druckenmiller/Burry (buy-side) actively shorting or exiting mega-cap tech; Visser (sell-side) overweighting Nvidia/Oracle/Amazon as secular compounders.
aligned: Hartnett and Dalio/Tudor Jones all independently invoke 2000 dot-com parallel — broad bubble consensus while market continues to rally.
Gundlach (buy-side) and Hartnett/Sonders (sell-side) all flag inflation and rate-path risk as the primary equity headwind entering H2 2026.
Druckenmiller, Berezin, and Hartnett all separately fade hyperscaler/mega-cap AI valuations — unusual buy/sell-side convergence on a crowded trade.
buy_side_trades: Short/reduce mega-cap tech (Nvidia short, Amazon/Microsoft cuts). Source: Burry, Druckenmiller, Tepper, Ackman.
Long Bitcoin and gold as inflation hedges. Source: Tudor Jones, Gundlach.
Long semis, AI infra, and AI memory over mega-cap software. Source: Druckenmiller, Tepper, Tudor Jones (IREN).
Long Argentine equities and copper as EM/commodity macro plays. Source: Druckenmiller.
sell_side_trades: Caution on broad equities; favour dividend/quality names over speculative growth. Source: Hartnett (BofA), Sonders (Schwab).
Long USD as most unexpected macro bet. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Long US equities vs European equities on divergent growth trajectories. Source: Hatzius (GS).
Long duration bonds given rate-hike probability near zero. Source: Hatzius (GS), Wilson (MS) post-rate clarity.
dominant_themes: Mega-cap tech rotation into semis, infra, and value
Geopolitical risk premium (Iran) as fragile equity tailwind

buy_side_bullets: Druckenmiller cut Amazon 94%, rotating into semis, copper, Argentina — AI mega-cap crowded.
Dalio: US equities near 1929/2000 bubble extremes; debt unsustainable; shift to boring fundamentals.
Tudor Jones: S&P buyers risk decade of losses; Bitcoin and AI infra are the only longs worth owning.
Gundlach: Warsh is no dove — 1970s inflation replay makes rate cuts unjustifiable; stay short duration.
Burry shorting Nvidia, long undervalued healthcare; growth/tech disconnect from fundamentals extreme.
sell_side_bullets: Hartnett (BofA): biggest bubble since railroads, only 4% of S&P at new highs — echoes March 2000.
Rosenberg: stagflation arriving — inflation up, growth down, large economy segments in deep pain.
Reid (DB): '1999 meets 1990' — tech exuberance layered on recession risk; crash parallels flashing.
Slok (Apollo): economy running hot, zero evidence AI kills jobs — bull case intact, no cuts needed.
Yardeni: FEMO not FOMO — earnings momentum drives rally, not bubble; bull case intact through 2028.
whats_new: Druckenmiller made a decisive 94% Amazon cut this week — clearest mega-cap tech exit signal of the period.
Gundlach explicitly reframed Fed narrative: Warsh as 1970s inflation hawk, displacing AI as the dominant macro story.
Hartnett introduced 'railroad bubble' framing and flagged an imminent June-specific catalyst threat to the rally.
Dalio escalated from cautious to outright 1929/2000 bubble comparison, adding bond market stress warning.
vs_market_buy: Dalio/Tudor Jones call bubble extremes while market trades near all-time highs and implied vol remains suppressed.
Gundlach prices in persistent high rates; market still discounts meaningful 2026 Fed easing.
Burry short Nvidia while consensus prices AI capex supercycle with no FCF ceiling in sight.
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg warns stagflation; credit spreads sit near 2-year tights, implying no growth scare priced.
Hartnett flags 4% breadth and bubble parallels while headline indices trade at or near record levels.
Berezin fades hyperscaler FCF projections; market prices $700B free cash flow by 2030 as base case.
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Slok (sell-side, bullish) says economy hot and AI creates jobs; Gundlach (buy-side, bearish) says that same heat makes cuts impossible and risks a hard landing.
Visser (sell-side) overweights Nvidia/Oracle on AI capex cycle; Burry (buy-side) is actively short Nvidia on valuation disconnect.
Yardeni (sell-side) calls gains FEMO-driven and healthy; Dalio/Tudor Jones (buy-side) call same gains bubble speculation requiring risk reduction.
aligned: Dalio + Hartnett + Reid + Tudor Jones all independently invoke dot-com/1929 bubble parallels — market is still rallying through their warnings.
Druckenmiller + Tepper + Ackman all rotating within tech rather than exiting equities — consensus that mega-cap software loses to AI infra/semis.
Gundlach + Reid both flag Fed hawkishness and sticky inflation as the structural macro override above AI narrative.
buy_side_trades: Long semiconductors, short/reduce mega-cap software (Amazon, Microsoft). Source: Druckenmiller, Tepper, Burry.
Long Bitcoin and gold as inflation hedges. Source: Tudor Jones, Gundlach.
Long Argentine equities and copper as EM/commodity macro rotation. Source: Druckenmiller.
Long AI infrastructure (IREN, memory stocks) over hyperscalers. Source: Tudor Jones, Tepper.
sell_side_trades: Stronger US dollar positioning amid bubble unwind risk. Source: Hartnett (BofA).
Long US equities vs European equities on divergent growth. Source: Hatzius (GS), Yardeni.
Long duration bonds given rate hike probability near zero. Source: Hatzius (GS), Wilson (MS).
Fade hyperscaler valuations on unrealistic FCF projections. Source: Berezin (BCA).
dominant_themes: AI bubble valuation vs earnings momentum — market's defining fault line
Fed path under Warsh — hawkish inflation regime replacing easing expectations
Mega-cap tech rotation — semis and AI infra replacing software as preferred exposure
Geopolitical risk premium — Iran conflict as transitory inflation shock vs tail risk
